req.title: Lab order routing (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers lab order routing with three distinct test concerns: (1) happy-path routing to a patient's preferred lab when available (EP - valid partition), (2) fallback routing when the preferred lab is unavailable (NEG/DT - error pathway), and (3) boundary values on timestamps and numeric fields such as epoch-zero, max-safe integer quantity, and exact midnight timestamps (BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'draft' through 'active' to 'completed' order status (STC). Together these satisfy the minimum positive/negative/boundary requirement while applying all mandated heuristics. Each test posts to /ServiceRequest on the fhir-lite target, uses a clinician auth fixture, and verifies routing and status independently.

tests.name: test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab set to 'lab-preferred-001'
Lab 'lab-preferred-001' is marked available in the system
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with a valid lab order payload referencing the patient and their preferred lab
expected: Response body contains performer reference matching 'lab-preferred-001'
trace: R-HLT-043: order routed to preferred lab when available
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available(clinician_auth_headers, preferred_lab_patient_fixture):
    patient_id = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["patient_id"]
    preferred_lab = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["preferred_lab"]  # 'lab-preferred-001'

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "performer": [{"reference": f"Organization/{preferred_lab}"}],
        "occurrenceDateTime": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
    }

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert body["intent"] == "order"
    performer_refs = [p["reference"] for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert f"Organization/{preferred_lab}" in performer_refs

tests.name: test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab set to 'lab-unavailable-999'
Lab 'lab-unavailable-999' is marked unavailable/inactive in the system
System default lab is configured as 'lab-default-001'
actions: POST /ServiceRequest referencing the patient whose preferred lab is unavailable
expected: Response contains an extension or note indicating fallback routing was applied
trace: R-HLT-043: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
DEFAULT_LAB = "lab-default-001"
UNAVAILABLE_LAB = "lab-unavailable-999"

def test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable(clinician_auth_headers, unavailable_lab_patient_fixture):
    patient_id = unavailable_lab_patient_fixture["patient_id"]

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "performer": [{"reference": f"Organization/{UNAVAILABLE_LAB}"}],
        "occurrenceDateTime": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
    }

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    performer_refs = [p["reference"] for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert f"Organization/{UNAVAILABLE_LAB}" not in performer_refs
    assert f"Organization/{DEFAULT_LAB}" in performer_refs
    assert body["status"] == "active"

tests.name: test_order_boundary_timestamp_epoch_zero_and_max_quantity
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab available
System must accept FHIR dateTime values and integer quantities
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with occurrenceDateTime set to epoch zero (1970-01-01T00:00:00Z)
Include quantity.value at the maximum safe boundary (2147483647)
Capture response and inspect validation outcome
expected: HTTP 201 or 422 with explicit validation error (system must not crash or return 500)
If 201: response contains the submitted timestamp unchanged
If 422: response OperationOutcome identifies the offending field by path
No 5xx server error under any boundary input
trace: R-HLT-043: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
EPOCH_ZERO = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"
MAX_INT32 = 2147483647

def test_order_boundary_timestamp_epoch_zero_and_max_quantity(clinician_auth_headers, preferred_lab_patient_fixture):
    patient_id = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["patient_id"]
    preferred_lab = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["preferred_lab"]

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "performer": [{"reference": f"Organization/{preferred_lab}"}],
        "occurrenceDateTime": EPOCH_ZERO,
        "quantityQuantity": {"value": MAX_INT32, "unit": "mL"}
    }

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (201, 422), (
        f"Expected 201 or 422 at boundary inputs, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    if resp.status_code == 201:
        body = resp.json()
        assert body.get("occurrenceDateTime") == EPOCH_ZERO
    else:
        body = resp.json()
        assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_order_status_transitions_draft_to_active_to_completed
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab available
System supports ServiceRequest status transitions: draft -> active -> completed
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with status 'draft' to create the order
expected: First PUT returns HTTP 200 with status 'active'
Second PUT returns HTTP 200 with status 'completed'
Final GET confirms status is 'completed' and order is traceable
trace: R-HLT-043: status tracked across order lifecycle
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_status_transitions_draft_to_active_to_completed(clinician_auth_headers, preferred_lab_patient_fixture):
    patient_id = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["patient_id"]
    preferred_lab = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["preferred_lab"]

    base_payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
        "performer": [{"reference": f"Organization/{preferred_lab}"}],
        "occurrenceDateTime": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
    }

    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest",
        json={**base_payload, "status": "draft"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    order_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    assert create_resp.json()["status"] == "draft"

    activate_resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{order_id}",
        json={**base_payload, "id": order_id, "status": "active"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert activate_resp.status_code == 200
    assert activate_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

    complete_resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{order_id}",
        json={**base_payload, "id": order_id, "status": "completed"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert complete_resp.status_code == 200
    assert complete_resp.json()["status"] == "completed"

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{order_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "completed"

tests.name: test_order_routing_decision_table_preferred_vs_default_lab
preconditions: Two patients exist: one with preferred lab available, one without any preferred lab configured
actions: POST /ServiceRequest for patient WITH a preferred lab available
POST /ServiceRequest for patient WITHOUT a preferred lab configured
Compare performer references in both responses
expected: Order for patient with preferred lab routes to that preferred lab
Order for patient without preferred lab routes to the system default lab 'lab-default-001'
Both orders return HTTP 201 and status 'active'
trace: R-HLT-043: order routed; preferred lab when available; fallback to default
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
DEFAULT_LAB = "lab-default-001"

def test_order_routing_decision_table_preferred_vs_default_lab(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    preferred_lab_patient_fixture,
    no_preferred_lab_patient_fixture
):
    def post_order(patient_id, performer_ref):
        payload = {
            "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
            "status": "active",
            "intent": "order",
            "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
            "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2160-0"}]},
            "occurrenceDateTime": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        }
        if performer_ref:
            payload["performer"] = [{"reference": performer_ref}]
        return requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)

    preferred_patient_id = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["patient_id"]
    preferred_lab = preferred_lab_patient_fixture["preferred_lab"]
    resp_preferred = post_order(preferred_patient_id, f"Organization/{preferred_lab}")
    assert resp_preferred.status_code == 201
    refs_preferred = [p["reference"] for p in resp_preferred.json().get("performer", [])]
    assert f"Organization/{preferred_lab}" in refs_preferred

    no_pref_patient_id = no_preferred_lab_patient_fixture["patient_id"]
    resp_default = post_order(no_pref_patient_id, None)
    assert resp_default.status_code == 201
    refs_default = [p["reference"] for p in resp_default.json().get("performer", [])]
    assert f"Organization/{DEFAULT_LAB}" in refs_default
